tool_name: IEC 60364 Three-Phase Cable Section Sizing & Voltage Drop

inputs.name: Load Power (P)
inputs.hint: Motor: from nameplate | Lighting: from lumen design
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: System Voltage (UL-L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Line Voltage Numeric Value (UL-L)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Conductor Material
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Selected Cable Cross-Section (S)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Installation Method
options: B1 - Single-core cables in open conduit
B2 - Multi-core cable in open conduit
E - Multicore cable in free air
F - Single-core cables in horizontal cable tray
G - Single-core cables in vertical cable tray
inputs.hint: IEC 60364-5-52 installation method — affects thermal ampacity
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Ambient Temperature
inputs.hint: Standard reference rating is at 30°C — derating factor applies above it

inputs.name: Load Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: Load_Current_A = (load_kw * 1000 * demand_factor) / (SQRT(3) * voltage_v * power_factor)   // [A] Line current — 3-Phase load
Resistance_R_ohm = (Resistivity * cable_length_m) / cross_section_val   // [ohm] Phase conductor resistance (one-way)
Total_Loop_R = 2 * Resistance_R_ohm   // [ohm] Go and return path loop resistance
VD_Voltage = SQRT(3) * Load_Current_A * (Resistance_R_ohm * power_factor + Reactance_X_ohm * Sin_Phi)   // [V] Voltage drop — 3-Phase | Source: IEC 60364-5-52
Voltage_At_Load = voltage_v - VD_Voltage   // [V] Line voltage at load terminal
Power_Loss_W = 3 * POWER(Load_Current_A, 2) * Resistance_R_ohm   // [W] Cable heat dissipation loss (3-phase)
Recommended_Min_Section = (SQRT(3) * Load_Current_A * Resistivity * cable_length_m * power_factor) / (max_vd_pct / 100 * voltage_v)   // [mm2] Minimum required cross-section based on voltage drop

power_factor_range.error_msg: Power factor must be between 0.5 and 1.0. Typically 0.8-0.9 for inductive loads. Below 0.5 is not practical.

voltage_current_match.error_msg: Current > 1000A requires a minimum cross-section of 70mm² to prevent thermal overload.

section_minimum.error_msg: IEC 60228: Power circuit minimum cross-section is 1.5mm². Smaller sizes are non-standard.

length_resistance.error_msg: Long run + small cross-section: for lines >500m, voltage drop and reactance effects are critical. Evaluate cross-section increase or voltage level upgrade.

smart_warnings.message: VOLTAGE DROP LIMIT EXCEEDED: Motor will draw excessive current at this low voltage -> overheat -> melt insulation -> cause short circuit. Increase cross-section by at least one standard size.
Voltage drop close to limit: Within 80% of limit. Cross-section upgrade is recommended to prevent exceeding limit on load surges or hot weather.
Cable heat dissipation loss > 3%: Annual energy cost is significant. Investment in cross-section upgrade typically pays back in 2-3 years. Run economic sizing optimization (Kelvin's Law).
High ambient temperature: Cable ampacity derates. Apply correction factor (Ct) for ambient >40°C per IEC 60364. Standard catalog ratings assume 30°C.
Selected cross-section is insufficient: does not meet voltage drop limits. Calculated minimum cross-section is displayed. Choose next standard size up.
High current (>630A): Consider parallel cables or busbar duct system. Parallel cable routing can be complex; busduct is more reliable.

fmea.effect: Power factor must be between 0.5 and 1.0. Typically 0.8-0.9 for inductive loads. Below 0.5 is not practical.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: VOLTAGE DROP LIMIT EXCEEDED: Motor will draw excessive current at this low voltage -> overheat -> melt insulation -> cause short circuit. Increase cross-section by at least one standard size.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: VD_Percent > max_vd_pct

fmea.failureMode: Voltage drop close to limit: Within 80% of limit. Cross-section upgrade is recommended to prevent exceeding limit on load surges or hot weather.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: VD_Percent > max_vd_pct * 0.8 && VD_Percent <= max_vd_pct

fmea.failureMode: Cable heat dissipation loss > 3%: Annual energy cost is significant. Investment in cross-section upgrade typically pays back in 2-3 years. Run economic sizing optimization (Kelvin's Law).
High ambient temperature: Cable ampacity derates. Apply correction factor (Ct) for ambient >40°C per IEC 60364. Standard catalog ratings assume 30°C.
Selected cross-section is insufficient: does not meet voltage drop limits. Calculated minimum cross-section is displayed. Choose next standard size up.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cross_section_val < Recommended_Min_Section

fmea.failureMode: High current (>630A): Consider parallel cables or busbar duct system. Parallel cable routing can be complex; busduct is more reliable.
